content.problemStatement:
Chef is making a dish that consists of exactly two ingredients. He has four ingredients A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C and DDD with tastiness a,b,c,a, b, c,a,b,c, and ddd respectively. He can use either AAA or BBB as the first ingredient and either CCC or DDD as the second ingredient.

The tastiness of a dish is the sum of tastiness of its ingredients. Find the **maximum** possible tastiness of the dish that the chef can prepare.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers a,b,c,a, b, c,a,b,c, and ddd — the tastiness of the four ingredients.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible tastiness of the dish that chef can prepare.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers a, b, c and d — the tastiness values of ingredients A, B, C and D respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum possible tastiness of the dish.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: max(3,5)=5 and max(6,2)=6, sum=11. Test case 2: max(16,15)=16 and max(5,4)=5, sum=21.

solution.approach: For each test case we have two independent choices: the first ingredient can be either A (tastiness a) or B (tastiness b); the second ingredient can be either C (tastiness c) or D (tastiness d). To maximise the sum we simply pick the larger value from each pair. Hence the answer is max(a, b) + max(c, d). This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        a = int(next(it)); b = int(next(it)); c = int(next(it)); d = int(next(it))
        out.append(str(max(a, b) + max(c, d)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
